reasoning: To achieve a positive resolution, the number of 'Strategic developments' in Burkina Faso must hit over 10 times the historical 30-day average. Given the base rate of ~21 events per month, a 10x spike requires at least 218 events in a single month. This would represent an unprecedented explosion of events (more than 7 per day), which is extremely rare in ACLED methodology unless there is a sweeping nationwide data dump or an extreme conflict anomaly like widespread documented looting or checkpoints in hundreds of individual villages. Furthermore, the junta's ongoing crackdown on local media makes an explosive 10x increase in event reporting even less likely. A foiled coup in April 2025 may produce a modest spike in arrests, but nowhere near the >218 distinct events required. I assign minimal probability to a 10x spike, slightly increasing over time to reflect the general uncertainty of longer horizons.
